title: ConcertProgramme
description: Structured metadata extracted from a single scanned concert programme image. A programme contains one or more concert notices (full programmes and/or brief notices of future concerts); each is captured as one entry in 'concerts'. This schema mirrors the hierarchical concert-level (c_*) / work-level (w_*) / movement-level (m_*) design described in the DLfM 2026 paper (Table 2, Figure: schema tree).

concerts.title: Concerts
concerts.description: Concert events printed on this programme image, in printed order. At least one. Treat each printed concert notice -- full programme or brief future-concert notice -- as its own entry; never mix details between concerts on the same image.

ConcertEvent.title: ConcertEvent
ConcertEvent.description: A single concert event (concert-level metadata, c_*), with its performed works.

c_position.title: Concert order
c_position.description: Order of this concert on the programme image, starting at 1.

c_title.title: Concert title
c_title.description: Event title, if printed (e.g. a named concert or recital title). As printed; do not infer one if absent.

c_date.title: Date
c_date.description: Concert date, normalised to ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is one of the two strictly-normalised fields (see prompts/PROMPT.txt rule 3).

c_time.title: Time
c_time.description: Concert start time, normalised to 24-hour ISO 8601 (HH:MM). Assume PM if AM/PM is missing; add ':00' if only an hour is printed.

c_venue.title: Venue
c_venue.description: Venue name or address line as printed.

c_society.title: Society / club
c_society.description: Organising society or club name as printed (e.g. 'Oxford University Musical Club').

c_series_no.title: Series / meeting no.
c_series_no.description: Series or meeting number as printed (e.g. '1100th Meeting').

c_comp_list.title: Composers (concert-level)
c_comp_list.description: Composers listed in a dedicated concert-level block only -- never aggregated from individual works below. Null if no such block is printed.

c_perf_list.title: Performers (concert-level)
c_perf_list.description: Performers listed in a dedicated concert-level block only -- never aggregated from individual works below. Null if no such block is printed.

works.title: Works
works.description: Works performed in this concert, in performance order. At least one.

Work.title: Work
Work.description: A single musical work performed within a concert (work-level metadata, w_*), with optional movements and work-level performers.

w_position.title: Work order
w_position.description: Order of this work within the concert, starting at 1.

w_title.title: Work title
w_title.description: Work title line, including key, opus, or catalogue number as printed (e.g. 'String Quartet in D minor, D 810').

w_comp.title: Composer
w_comp.description: Composer attributed to this work. Almost every work should have one; only use null if truly absent from the print.

w_movements.title: Movements
w_movements.description: Movements or sections printed under this work, in order. Do not split a work into separate 'works' entries unless it has distinct opus/catalogue numbers -- see prompts/PROMPT.txt rule 6. Null if none are listed.

w_perf_list.title: Performers (work-level)
w_perf_list.description: Performers printed directly beneath this specific work only (not the concert-level block). Null if none are listed.

Movement.title: Movement
Movement.description: A movement or section heading printed under a work (movement-level metadata, m_*).

m_position.title: Movement order
m_position.description: Order of this movement within the work, starting at 1.

m_title.title: Movement / section heading
m_title.description: Movement or section heading as printed (e.g. 'Allegro molto moderato').

Performer.title: Performer
Performer.description: A named performer with optional role label(s).

name.title: Name
name.description: Performer name as printed (e.g. 'S. F. Blagrove', or an ensemble name).

roles.title: Role(s)
roles.description: Instrument, voice, or role label(s) for this performer (e.g. ['Violin', 'Leader']).
